confirmed.title: widgets README still calls package a 'Phase 2 scaffold' with concrete widget types unimplemented
confirmed.suggested_fix: Rewrite the Status block to state the four concrete widget types and the dashboard/widget-data controllers are shipped (WIDGETS-001..009), mirroring SKILL.md.
confirmed.detail: README Status says: "Phase 2 scaffold (WIDGETS-001). Concrete widget types (StatWidget, ChartWidget, TableWidget, CustomWidget) and the dashboard controllers land in WIDGETS-002..006." All of those exist on disk: packages/widgets/src/StatWidget.php, ChartWidget.php, TableWidget.php, CustomWidget.php, plus Http/Controllers/DashboardController.php and WidgetDataController.php. The canonical SKILL.md (same package) already documents WIDGETS-001..009/013 as delivered (~154 Pest tests). The README's forward-looking 'land in WIDGETS-002..006' is false.
confirmed.why_real: The README at packages/widgets/README.md:7 states "Phase 2 scaffold (WIDGETS-001). Concrete widget types (StatWidget, ChartWidget, TableWidget, CustomWidget) and the dashboard controllers land in WIDGETS-002..006." This forward-looking framing (will "land in") is objectively false vs the code on disk: all four widget classes exist (src/StatWidget.php, ChartWidget.php, TableWidget.php, CustomWidget.php) and both controllers exist (src/Http/Controllers/DashboardController.php, WidgetDataController.php). The same package's canonical SKILL.md documents WIDGETS-002..009 as DELIVERED with ~154 passing Pest tests, confirming the features are implemented, not pending. The README is in-scope (a package README, not PLANNING/docs/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). The ledger's only widgets entry concerns installation.md's meta-package list, a different finding; this "Phase 2 scaffold / land in WIDGETS-002..006" claim is not ledgered. Both sides confirmed: doc says not-yet-built, disk shows built.

confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. packages/fields-advanced/README.md:7 states "Phase 2 scaffold (FIELDS-ADV-001). The first concrete type, RichTextField, ships with FIELDS-ADV-002. Remaining types land in FIELDS-ADV-003+." On disk, packages/fields-advanced/src/Types/ contains all 8 types as concrete `final class ... extends Field` implementations (120-219 lines each): RichTextField, MarkdownField, CodeField, RepeaterField, BuilderField, KeyValueField, TagsField, WizardField. The README's "scaffold / remaining types not yet landed" framing is objectively false vs current code. The doc is in-scope (package README, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). The ledger contains the JS twin (packages-js/fields-advanced/README.md, line 171) and the PHP SKILL.md, but NOT this PHP README (packages/fields-advanced/README.md) — so it is not a duplicate. The claim is checkable and genuinely contradicted.

confirmed.title: mcp README says JSON-RPC handler and tool/resource/prompt registries are still to be delivered
confirmed.suggested_fix: Reword Status to say the JSON-RPC handler, registries and panel autoloading are shipped (4 tools/1 resource/2 prompts), and scope the remaining work to only the `arqel:mcp:serve` Artisan command wrapper.
confirmed.detail: README says: "Phase 2 scaffold (MCP-001). The JSON-RPC handler, serve Artisan command, tool/resource/prompt registries, and panel autoloading land in MCP-002..010." In reality McpServer.php implements the JSON-RPC 2.0 dispatch (handleRequest) plus registerTool/registerResource/registerPrompt and a serve()/serveStreams() loop; McpServiceProvider.php auto-registers 4 tools, 1 resource and 2 prompts. So the handler, registries and panel autoloading ARE shipped. (Only the `arqel:mcp:serve` Artisan command genuinely does not exist yet — SKILL.md notes this correctly.) The blanket 'land in MCP-002..010' is misleading for everything except the Artisan wrapper.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides on disk. packages/mcp/README.md:7 states "Phase 2 scaffold (MCP-001). The JSON-RPC handler, `serve` Artisan command, tool/resource/prompt registries, and panel autoloading land in MCP-002..010." — framing all of these as not-yet-delivered future work. Reality: src/McpServer.php implements handleRequest() (JSON-RPC 2.0 dispatch, line 174), registerTool/registerResource/registerPrompt (lines 69/90/110), and serve()/serveStreams() (lines 233/255). src/McpServiceProvider.php auto-registers 4 tools (registerTool x4) plus a resource (auto-registered) and 2 prompts (registerPrompt x2) — i.e., the handler, registries, and panel/skill autoloading ARE shipped. SKILL.md corroborates that MCP-002..009 are delivered and explicitly notes only that the `arqel:mcp:serve` Artisan command does not yet exist. So the README's blanket "land in MCP-002..010" is objectively false/misleading for everything except the Artisan command wrapper. The file is in-scope (a package README, not PLANNING/CHANGELOG/tickets/generated) and is not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json.

So labeling DEVTOOLS-002..004 as "por vir" and the package as a mere "Scaffold (DEVTOOLS-001)" is factually wrong. In-scope (package README, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated). Confirmed both sides on disk.

DOC SIDE (apps/docs/advanced/dashboards.md:145, in-scope — it's a published guide, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG): The FAQ "Where do React components live?" answers "@arqel-dev/ui/widgets — delivered in WIDGETS-010..012 (currently in development; the PHP side and Inertia payload are already stable)." confirmed.title: command-palette guide references a non-existent CommandPaletteServiceProvider class (EN)
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace 'CommandPaletteServiceProvider' with 'ArqelServiceProvider' (or 'the arqel-dev/core service provider').
confirmed.detail: Doc says: "The CommandPaletteServiceProvider ships with arqel-dev/core and registers the built-in providers automatically." No such class exists; packages/core/src has only ArqelServiceProvider.php (the sole *ServiceProvider in core). The command-palette pieces (CommandRegistry, FuzzyMatcher, Command, NavigationCommandProvider, ThemeCommandProvider, CommandPaletteController) live under src/CommandPalette/ and src/Http/Controllers/, registered by ArqelServiceProvider.
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. Doc apps/docs/advanced/command-palette.md:15 states: "The CommandPaletteServiceProvider ships with arqel-dev/core and registers the built-in providers automatically." This is objectively false. grep for "CommandPaletteServiceProvider" across packages/core/src returns nothing (exit 1); the only *ServiceProvider in core is ArqelServiceProvider.php. The command-palette pieces live in src/CommandPalette/ (Command, CommandRegistry, FuzzyMatcher, Providers/{NavigationCommandProvider,ThemeCommandProvider}). Registration of the CommandRegistry singleton and the two built-in providers is done by ArqelServiceProvider (line 68 singleton, lines 226-227 registerProvider). So the named class is fabricated and the registration attribution is wrong — a checkable factual contradiction. Doc is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated) and not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (grep exit 1).

Grepping packages/ for CommandPaletteServiceProvider returns zero matches. confirmed.title: dashboards guide lists 'trend' as a StatWidget setter
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace the setter list with the real StatWidget API (value/descriptionIcon/icon/color) and note 'trend' is supplied via the data() array, not a setter.
confirmed.detail: Doc: "### StatWidget (KPI card) — Setters: value/description/icon/color/trend." StatWidget.php defines value(), descriptionIcon(), color(), icon() (and inherits heading/description/columnSpan/poll/deferred/canSee from Widget). There is no trend() setter anywhere in packages/widgets/src; 'trend' is only a key inside the data() array (Widget.php comment: ['value' => $count, 'trend' => '+12%']). Listing trend as a setter is incorrect; descriptionIcon is also the actual method name (not 'description' on StatWidget).
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. apps/docs/advanced/dashboards.md:68 states StatWidget setters are `value/description/icon/color/trend`. Reading packages/widgets/src/StatWidget.php shows the actual fluent setters are value(), statDescription(), descriptionIcon(), color(), icon(), chart(), url() (plus inherited heading/description/columnSpan/poll/deferred/canSee from Widget). A grep for `function trend` / `->trend(` across packages/widgets/src returns nothing — there is NO trend() setter. `trend` exists only as a key inside data() arrays in examples (Widget.php). Listing `trend` as a StatWidget setter is objectively false/misleading. The doc is in-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/CHANGELOG/generated) and not in the ledger (the only dashboards.md:line-3 ledger entry concerns relative-link depth, a different issue). Note: the finding's secondary claim that `description` is wrong is itself imprecise — description() IS a valid inherited setter from Widget — but the primary defect (trend as a nonexistent setter) stands and is enough to confirm a real factual-drift bug.

confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. confirmed.title: Docs reference nonexistent artisan command `arqel:form-request`
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace `php artisan arqel:form-request` / `php artisan arqel:form-request PostResource` with `php artisan arqel:resource <Model> --with-form-requests` (the only command that drives FormRequestGenerator). Applies to fields.md:81 and tables-forms.md:141.
confirmed.detail: fields.md:81 states the FormRequestGenerator is invoked via `php artisan arqel:form-request`, and tables-forms.md:141 says `php artisan arqel:form-request PostResource` generates the Store/Update requests. No such command exists: grepping `$signature` across all packages/*/src yields arqel:install, arqel:resource, arqel:make-user, arqel:doctor, arqel:introspect, arqel:audit, arqel:cloud:info, arqel:pulse:info, arqel:field, arqel:widget, arqel:dashboard, arqel:versions:prune, arqel:marketplace:*, arqel:plugin:list, arqel:tenant:* — but NOT arqel:form-request. The FormRequestGenerator class (packages/form/src/FormRequestGenerator.php) exists but is only wired into the resource generator: the real mechanism is `php artisan arqel:resource <Model> --with-form-requests` (confirmed by MakeResourceCommand signature and core/SKILL.md line 343).
confirmed.why_real: CONFIRMED. Doc side: apps/docs/guide/fields.md:81 states FormRequestGenerator is invoked via `php artisan arqel:form-request`; apps/docs/guide/tables-forms.md:141 states `php artisan arqel:form-request PostResource` generates StorePostRequest/UpdatePostRequest. Code side: grepping every command `$signature` across packages/*/src yields arqel:marketplace:*, arqel:field, arqel:introspect, arqel:pulse:info, arqel:widget, arqel:make-user, arqel:plugin:list, arqel:tenant:*, arqel:resource, arqel:install, arqel:cloud:info, arqel:doctor, arqel:audit, arqel:versions:prune, arqel:dashboard — but NO arqel:form-request. The FormRequestGenerator class exists at packages/form/src/FormRequestGenerator.php but is only wired into MakeResourceCommand (packages/core/src/Commands/MakeResourceCommand.php), invoked via the `--with-form-requests` flag of `arqel:resource` (confirmed at lines 43, 60, 96). So the real command is `php artisan arqel:resource <Model> --with-form-requests`, not the nonexistent `arqel:form-request`. Doc is in-scope (apps/docs/guide, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated), claim is objectively checkable, contradicts current code, and is not in the ledger (grep of reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json returned no match). Two doc locations affected: fields.md:81 and tables-forms.md:141.

confirmed.title: realtime JS SKILL.md lists three nonexistent hooks
confirmed.suggested_fix: Remove `useResourcePresence`, `useActionProgress`, and `useWidgetRealtime` from the hook list at packages-js/realtime/SKILL.md:33-34 (keep useResourceUpdates), or replace with the hooks that actually exist.
confirmed.detail: packages-js/realtime/SKILL.md:33-34 names the hooks that consume the channel types as `useResourceUpdates`, `useResourcePresence`, `useActionProgress`, `useWidgetRealtime`. Of these, only useResourceUpdates exists (in @arqel-dev/hooks). Grepping all packages-js/*/src for useResourcePresence, useActionProgress, useWidgetRealtime returns nothing — they exist in no JS package. @arqel-dev/hooks exports exactly 11 hooks (useAction, useArqelForm, useBreakpoint, useCanAccess, useFieldDependencies, useFlash, useNavigation, useArqelOptimistic, useResource, useResourceUpdates, useTable) and @arqel-dev/realtime exports only useConnectionStatus + useFallbackPolling.
